mechanism: When no built-in policy provides the required effect, a custom definition is authored in customDefinitions/, declared in policyDefinitions.bicepparam, and referenced in initiative parameters as {{custom}}/name.
why: A whitelisted resource type with no Deny gate can be deployed in any configuration — the platform approves the type but cannot enforce the posture. Audit-only built-in policies observe violations; they do not prevent them.

related.note: Whitelisting a type with a known Deny gap requires a custom definition before coverage is complete.
cae_publicNetworkAccess fills the gap where no built-in Deny existed for Container Apps environments.
Custom definitions provide Deny where built-in policies only offer Audit.
Custom definitions provide Deny where built-in policies only offer Audit.
Custom diagnostic definitions follow the same DeployIfNotExists pattern as built-in ones.
A compliant default does not substitute for a custom Deny definition.

violations: Resource type whitelisted with a known Deny gap and no custom definition to close it.
Custom definition not declared in policyDefinitions.bicepparam — not deployed.
Initiative parameter referencing a custom definition without the {{custom}}/ prefix — invalid policy ID at assignment time.
Custom definition deployed outside customPolicyDefinitions.bicep — not lifecycle-managed by the stack.
